demographics.occupation: reduced paid work hours; full-time unpaid family carer
demographics.living_situation: lives with and is the sole carer for a dependent family member

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I heard a pop in my back when I was lifting them, and now I can't stand up, but there is literally no one else to take care of them.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The back pain came on suddenly while I was transferring the person I care for, and now I physically can't get up — and there's no one else here to look after them.

symptom_narrative.onset: suddenly today, right when I felt a pop lifting the person I care for
symptom_narrative.duration: started a few hours ago and hasn't eased
symptom_narrative.character: severe cramping, gripping spasm across the lower back on both sides
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: so bad I can't stand, walk, or get off the floor
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: across the lower back, both sides
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, much worse with any movement
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any attempt to move, sit up, or stand
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: totally worn out and exhausted; a bit sweaty and clammy from the pain; heart feels like it's racing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: have had niggling back strain warning signs for months but ignored them and kept going
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely unable to stand, walk, or do any of the caring I normally do; stuck on the floor

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: no ongoing medical conditions that I know of; I haven't seen my own doctor in over three years

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I've been the sole unpaid carer for a marginalised family member for years — round-the-clock, barely any sleep, no breaks and no one to share it with. I've had to cut my paid work hours so money is tight. I feel guilty even though I do everything I can, and angry that there's no support out there. The person I care for is safe in their medical bed right now but they depend on me for everything, and we don't have a hoist or any lifting equipment at home.

objective_data_offered.value: lying flat on the floor, visibly grimacing, sweaty/clammy, and unable to stand up or bear weight
heart feels like it's racing and I'm sweaty, but I think that's from how much pain I'm in
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's subjective impression, no home device reading provided

objective_data_offered.value: the dependent family member is in a medical bed nearby but relies on me for all care; there is no hoist or mechanical transfer aid in the home
